    25â†’- **Teammate 1 (Local Context):** Informed the fraud risk calibration. Bonamoussadi's middle-class character, ~560 registered businesses, high mobile money kiosk density (100-200 agents), and seasonal flooding patterns all shape what "normal" agent behavior looks like versus anomalous patterns.
    26â†’- **Teammate 2 (System Design):** The confidence score system defined in Section 6 of the system design (recency 25%, sourceCount 20%, photoEvidence 20%, gpsAccuracy 15%, reviewerApproval 10%, fieldCompleteness 10%) is the foundation. This document extends it with a dedicated **fraud risk score** that feeds into the confidence score as a penalty factor. The event-sourced `point_events` model, `SubmissionFraudCheck` interface, and `ProjectedPoint` projection engine are all preserved and extended.
    27â†’- **Teammate 5 (Cybersecurity):** The threat model (T4: GPS Spoofing at risk 16/25, T2: Agent Impersonation at 12/25) and the existing `submissionFraud.ts` implementation (EXIF extraction, haversine distance, IP geolocation) are the starting point. This document fills the gaps identified by Teammate 5: no velocity limiting, no photo deduplication, no user trust scoring, no temporal analysis, no duplicate POI detection, no device fingerprint cross-referencing, no field plausibility validation, and XP awarded before validation.
    28â†’- **Existing Fraud Strategy (docs/team/05-fraud-strategy.md):** The fraud scenario catalog (F-FAB-01 through F-POI-02), detection rules (R-VEL-01 through R-GRA-01), risk scoring model (LocationRisk, PhotoRisk, TemporalRisk, UserRisk, BehavioralRisk), response playbooks (P-FAB through P-POI), and false positive minimization strategies are all incorporated and operationalized here.
    29â†’- **Database Migration (20260305_fraud_analytics_foundation.sql):** The `submission_image_hashes`, `device_user_map`, `fraud_audit_log`, `admin_reviews`, and `analytics_weekly` tables plus materialized views (`mv_point_summary`, `mv_user_activity`, `mv_daily_stats`) are assumed to be deployed.
    30â†’
    31â†’Where prior documents define *what* to detect, this document defines *how* to detect it at the algorithmic level, with pseudocode, formulas, decision trees, and operational procedures.
    32â†’

    34â†’
    35â†’## 1. Fraud Threat Landscape
    36â†’
    37â†’### 1.1 Threat Categorization Matrix
    38â†’
    39â†’#### Agent-Side Fraud
    40â†’
    41â†’| ID | Threat | Motivation | Method | Detection Difficulty | Business Impact | Likelihood |
    42â†’|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    43â†’| A1 | **Fabricated Submissions** | Earn XP (5 pts per CREATE_EVENT) without fieldwork | Submit plausible details + random storefront photo with GPS inside Bonamoussadi geofence (4.0755-4.0999 lat, 9.7185-9.76 lng) | High -- current system only validates field presence and geofence | **Critical** -- phantom POIs route users to nonexistent locations | High |
    44â†’| A2 | **Duplicate Entries** | Multiply XP from a single visit; drip-feed enrichments | Create a POI, then submit ENRICH_EVENTs for each gap field individually (pharmacy has 3 enrichable fields = 4 submissions = 20 XP from one location) | Low -- temporal clustering of same userId/pointId is visible | Medium -- inflates contribution counts, may or may not corrupt data | High |
    45â†’| A3 | **GPS Spoofing** | Submit from home without visiting locations | Use Fake GPS / Mock Locations app on Android; navigator.geolocation returns spoofed coords; if photo lacks EXIF GPS (common on feature phones), the cross-check produces null | Medium -- IP geolocation helps but VPN defeats it; current 50km IP threshold is too coarse | **Critical** -- fundamentally undermines ground-truth value proposition | High |
    46â†’| A4 | **Recycled / Stock Photos** | Avoid fieldwork; reuse one trip's photos across many submissions | Take one photo, submit as evidence for multiple POIs at slightly different coordinates; or download storefronts from Google Maps | Medium -- identical EXIF data across submissions is detectable, but stripped EXIF (screenshots/downloads) bypasses this | High -- multiple fake POIs backed by seemingly legitimate evidence | High |
    47â†’| A5 | **Collusion Between Agents** | Collectively inflate XP; dominate leaderboard for rewards | User A creates POI, User B enriches, User C enriches further; reverse roles; timed to avoid velocity flags | High -- individual patterns look normal; requires graph analysis of user-to-user and user-to-point relationships | High -- coordinated fraud at scale corrupts both data and incentive structure | Medium |
    48â†’| A6 | **Ghost Entities** | Create fictional businesses to pad submission counts | Fabricate business names, take photo of blank wall or unrelated storefront, assign plausible attributes | High -- without ground-truth cross-reference, a well-crafted ghost entity is indistinguishable from a real one | **Critical** -- dataset pollution; downstream consumers route users to nonexistent locations | Medium |
    49â†’| A7 | **Alt Account Farming** | Multiply XP, dominate multiple leaderboard slots, circumvent per-account limits | Register with different emails; current auth has no device-binding or phone verification; `deviceId` stored in localStorage is clearable | Medium -- same deviceId across userIds is a strong signal; same IP and device fingerprint also signals alt farming | High -- undermines one-person-one-account assumption | Medium |
    50â†’| A8 | **AI-Generated Photos** | Scale fabrication without physical presence | Generate pharmacy/kiosk images using diffusion models; no EXIF data; submit with fabricated coordinates within geofence | High -- AI images lack EXIF but so do many legitimate feature phone photos | **Critical** -- at scale, floods dataset with convincing but fictional POIs | Low (rising) |
    51â†’
    52â†’#### System-Side Fraud
    53â†’
    54â†’| ID | Threat | Motivation | Method | Detection Difficulty | Business Impact | Likelihood |
    55â†’|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    56â†’| S1 | **Data Manipulation by Insiders** | Financial gain (sell data), sabotage, or cover up agent fraud | Admin with Neon PostgreSQL access directly modifies `point_events` rows; bypasses event-sourced append-only model | Medium -- event-sourced model creates audit trail but direct DB access bypasses it; no dedicated security audit log exists | **Critical** -- corrupts the trusted dataset; damages all client relationships | Low |
    57â†’| S2 | **Unauthorized Bulk Exports** | Sell dataset to competitors; personal profit | Authenticated user queries `/api/submissions GET` without pagination limits; extracts entire Bonamoussadi dataset | Medium -- any authenticated user can query; no per-user export throttling beyond Vercel defaults | High -- competitor gains ADL data product without paying | Medium |
    58â†’| S3 | **Admin Approval Fraud** | Corrupt admin approves fraudulent submissions to benefit a specific agent | Admin marks fabricated submissions as "approved" in `admin_reviews` table; inflates agent's trust score and XP | High -- requires monitoring admin approval patterns; currently no admin action logging | High -- fraudulent data enters the published dataset with "admin approved" stamp | Low |
    59â†’
    60â†’#### Client-Side Fraud
    61â†’
    62â†’| ID | Threat | Motivation | Method | Detection Difficulty | Business Impact | Likelihood |
    63â†’|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    64â†’| C1 | **Misrepresentation of Data Quality** | Oversell to close deals; inflate perceived coverage | Present low-confidence data (score 30-50) as verified ground-truth to clients; cherry-pick high-quality samples while omitting data gaps | Medium -- requires comparing what was delivered versus actual dataset quality | **Critical** -- client discovers reality, destroys trust, demands refunds, shares negative reviews | Low |
    65â†’| C2 | **Selective Data Delivery** | Hide unfavorable data from specific clients | Exclude data points that show a client's competitor in a favorable light; or exclude points that reveal regulatory violations for a client's business | High -- requires intent analysis; difficult to distinguish from legitimate data filtering | High -- creates legal and ethical liability | Very Low |

    96â†’## 2. GPS Spoofing Detection
    97â†’
    98â†’### 2.1 Multi-Layered Detection Architecture
    99â†’
   100â†’GPS spoofing is ADL's highest-priority fraud vector (Teammate 5's T4: risk score 16/25). The current system checks EXIF GPS against submission GPS (1km threshold) and IP geolocation (50km threshold), but this is insufficient. The following multi-layered system addresses the gaps.
   101â†’
   102â†’```
   103â†’GPS SPOOFING DETECTION PIPELINE
   104â†’================================
   105â†’
   106â†’Layer 1: CLIENT-SIDE CHECKS (in PWA before submission)
   107â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Mock location API detection
   108â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Sensor data consistency (accelerometer, gyroscope)
   109â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ GPS accuracy metadata capture
   110â†’  â””â”€â”€ Network type recording
   111â†’
   112â†’Layer 2: SERVER-SIDE CHECKS (at submission time)
   113â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ EXIF GPS vs submission GPS (existing, 1km threshold)
   114â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ IP geolocation vs submission GPS (existing, 50km threshold)
   115â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Velocity analysis (impossible travel between submissions)
   116â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Cell tower / network type consistency
   117â†’  â””â”€â”€ Known location database cross-reference
   118â†’
   119â†’Layer 3: STATISTICAL ANALYSIS (batch, post-submission)
   120â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Agent movement pattern modeling
   121â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Impossible travel detection across sessions
   122â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ GPS coordinate clustering analysis
   123â†’  â”œâ”€â”€ Precision anomaly detection
   124â†’  â””â”€â”€ Collusion pattern detection
   125â†’
   126â†’Output: GPS Confidence Score (0-100)
   127â†’```
   128â†’
   129â†’### 2.2 Layer 1: Client-Side Checks
   130â†’
   131â†’These checks run in the PWA before submission. They cannot prevent a determined attacker (client-side checks are bypassable) but they raise the bar and collect signals for server-side analysis.
   132â†’
   133â†’```typescript
   134â†’// lib/client/gpsIntegrity.ts
   135â†’
   136â†’interface GpsIntegrityReport {
   137â†’  // Mock location detection
   138â†’  mockLocationDetected: boolean;
   139â†’  mockLocationMethod: string | null;
   140â†’
   141â†’  // Sensor consistency
   142â†’  hasAccelerometerData: boolean;
   143â†’  hasGyroscopeData: boolean;
   144â†’  accelerometerSampleCount: number;
   145â†’  motionDetectedDuringCapture: boolean;

   148â†’  gpsAccuracyMeters: number | null;
   149â†’  gpsProvider: string | null;  // 'gps' | 'network' | 'fused' | 'unknown'
   150â†’  gpsSatelliteCount: number | null;
   151â†’  gpsAltitudeMeters: number | null;

   154â†’

   157â†’  wifiSSID: string | null;     // if on wifi (may be null due to permissions)
   158â†’
   159â†’  // Timestamps
   160â†’  gpsTimestamp: number;        // from Position.timestamp
   161â†’  deviceTimestamp: number;     // Date.now() at capture
   162â†’  timeDeltaMs: number;         // |gpsTimestamp - deviceTimestamp|
   163â†’}
   164â†’
   165â†’async function collectGpsIntegrity(
   166â†’  position: GeolocationPosition
   167â†’): Promise<GpsIntegrityReport> {
   168â†’  const report: GpsIntegrityReport = {
   169â†’    mockLocationDetected: false,
   170â†’    mockLocationMethod: null,
   171â†’    hasAccelerometerData: false,
   172â†’    hasGyroscopeData: false,
   173â†’    accelerometerSampleCount: 0,
   174â†’    motionDetectedDuringCapture: false,
   175â†’    gpsAccuracyMeters: position.coords.accuracy ?? null,
   176â†’    gpsProvider: null,
   177â†’    gpsSatelliteCount: null,
   178â†’    gpsAltitudeMeters: position.coords.altitude ?? null,
   179â†’    gpsSpeedMps: position.coords.speed ?? null,
   180â†’    gpsHeading: position.coords.heading ?? null,
   181â†’    networkType: null,
   182â†’    wifiSSID: null,
   183â†’    gpsTimestamp: position.timestamp,
   184â†’    deviceTimestamp: Date.now(),
   185â†’    timeDeltaMs: Math.abs(Date.now() - position.timestamp),
   186â†’  };
   187â†’
   188â†’  // â”€â”€ MOCK LOCATION DETECTION â”€â”€
   189â†’  // Android exposes Position.isMocked in some browsers (Chrome 90+)
   190â†’  // This is the strongest client-side signal
   191â†’  const coords = position.coords as GeolocationCoordinates & {
   192â†’    isMocked?: boolean;
   193â†’  };

   210â†’  // GPS accuracy of exactly 0 or exactly 1 meter is unrealistic
   211â†’  // for consumer Android phones (typical: 3-30m)
   212â†’  if (position.coords.accuracy !== null &&
   213â†’      position.coords.accuracy !== undefined &&
   214â†’      position.coords.accuracy < 1) {
   215â†’    report.mockLocationDetected = true;
   216â†’    report.mockLocationMethod = 'Sub-meter accuracy on consumer device';
   217â†’  }
   218â†’
   219â†’  // â”€â”€ SENSOR DATA COLLECTION â”€â”€
   220â†’  // Collect 2 seconds of accelerometer data to verify physical movement
   221â†’  try {
   222â†’    const sensorData = await collectSensorData(2000);
   223â†’    report.hasAccelerometerData = sensorData.hasAccelerometer;
   224â†’    report.hasGyroscopeData = sensorData.hasGyroscope;
   225â†’    report.accelerometerSampleCount = sensorData.sampleCount;
   226â†’    report.motionDetectedDuringCapture = sensorData.motionDetected;
   227â†’  } catch {
   228â†’    // Sensor API not available -- common on low-end phones
   229â†’    // Not a fraud signal by itself
   230â†’  }
   231â†’
   232â†’  // â”€â”€ NETWORK TYPE â”€â”€
   233â†’  const connection = (navigator as Navigator & {
   234â†’    connection?: { effectiveType?: string; type?: string };
   235â†’  }).connection;
   236â†’  if (connection) {
   237â†’    report.networkType = connection.effectiveType ?? connection.type ?? 'unknown';
   238â†’  }
   239â†’
   240â†’  return report;
   241â†’}
   242â†’
   243â†’interface SensorSample {
   244â†’  x: number;
   245â†’  y: number;
   246â†’  z: number;
   247â†’  timestamp: number;
   248â†’}
   249â†’
   250â†’async function collectSensorData(durationMs: number): Promise<{
   251â†’  hasAccelerometer: boolean;
   252â†’  hasGyroscope: boolean;
   253â†’  sampleCount: number;
   254â†’  motionDetected: boolean;
   255â†’}> {
   256â†’  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   257â†’    const samples: SensorSample[] = [];
   258â†’    let hasAccel = false;
   259â†’    let hasGyro = false;
   260â†’
   261â†’    const handler = (event: DeviceMotionEvent) => {
   262â†’      if (event.accelerationIncludingGravity) {
   263â†’        hasAccel = true;
   264â†’        samples.push({
   265â†’          x: event.accelerationIncludingGravity.x ?? 0,
   266â†’          y: event.accelerationIncludingGravity.y ?? 0,
   267â†’          z: event.accelerationIncludingGravity.z ?? 0,
   268â†’          timestamp: Date.now(),
   269â†’        });
   270â†’      }
   271â†’      if (event.rotationRate) {
   272â†’        hasGyro = true;
   273â†’      }
   274â†’    };
   275â†’
   276â†’    window.addEventListener('devicemotion', handler);
   277â†’
   278â†’    setTimeout(() => {
   279â†’      window.removeEventListener('devicemotion', handler);
   280â†’
   281â†’      // Motion detection: check if accelerometer values vary
   282â†’      // A stationary spoofed device will show near-constant values
   283â†’      // A device being carried shows natural micro-vibrations
   284â†’      let motionDetected = false;
   285â†’      if (samples.length >= 5) {
   286â†’        const xValues = samples.map(s => s.x);
   287â†’        const variance = computeVariance(xValues);
   288â†’        // Walking produces variance > 0.1 m/s^2
   289â†’        // A phone on a desk: variance ~0.01-0.05
   290â†’        // Spoofed/emulator: variance ~0 or exactly constant
   291â†’        motionDetected = variance > 0.05;
   292â†’      }
   293â†’
   294â†’      resolve({
   295â†’        hasAccelerometer: hasAccel,
   296â†’        hasGyroscope: hasGyro,
   297â†’        sampleCount: samples.length,
   298â†’        motionDetected,
   299â†’      });
   300â†’    }, durationMs);

1â†’# ADL System Design: End-to-End Data Collection Workflow
     2â†’## Bonamoussadi, Douala, Cameroon
     3â†’
     4â†’**Teammate 2 -- System Design Expert Deliverable**
     5â†’**Date:** March 2026
     6â†’
     7â†’---
     8â†’
     9â†’## Table of Contents
    10â†’
    11â†’1. [Unified Data Model](#1-unified-data-model)
    12â†’2. [Entity ID Strategy](#2-entity-id-strategy)
    13â†’3. [Baseline vs Snapshot Logic](#3-baseline-vs-snapshot-logic)
    14â†’4. [Delta Computation Rules](#4-delta-computation-rules)
    15â†’5. [Collection Workflow](#5-collection-workflow)
    16â†’6. [Data Quality Scoring](#6-data-quality-scoring)
    17â†’
    18â†’---
    19â†’
    20â†’## Existing System Context
    21â†’
    22â†’Before proposing new designs, this document acknowledges what is already built and working. The current MVP includes:
    23â†’
    24â†’- **Event-sourced data model:** `point_events` table with `CREATE_EVENT` / `ENRICH_EVENT` types
    25â†’- **Point projection engine:** Events are folded into `ProjectedPoint` objects via `projectPointsFromEvents()`
    26â†’- **Snapshot/delta system:** Weekly snapshots frozen to `snapshots` table, deltas computed between consecutive snapshots, stats with z-score anomaly detection
    27â†’- **Fraud detection:** EXIF GPS extraction, haversine distance checks, IP geolocation cross-referencing
    28â†’- **Six active verticals:** `pharmacy`, `fuel_station`, `mobile_money`, `alcohol_outlet`, `retail_kiosk`, `billboard`
    29â†’- **Geofencing:** Bonamoussadi bounding box (4.0755-4.0999 N, 9.7185-9.7602 E)
    30â†’- **Offline queue:** Client-side submission queuing with sync
    31â†’- **Seed data:** OSM Overpass imports with external ID tracking
    32â†’
    33â†’The designs below extend this foundation to cover the full seven verticals (adding `transport_road` and `census_proxy`, replacing `retail_kiosk` as a standalone vertical), define precise schemas, and formalize the collection workflows needed for the Bonamoussadi pilot.
    34â†’
    35â†’---
    36â†’
    37â†’## 1. Unified Data Model
    38â†’
    39â†’### 1.1 Design Philosophy
    40â†’
    41â†’The existing `point_events` table uses a `details JSONB` column for vertical-specific fields. This is the correct pattern for ADL's stage -- it allows rapid vertical addition without schema migrations. The design below formalizes the structure inside that JSONB while keeping the flexible storage layer.
    42â†’
    43â†’The model follows three principles:
    44â†’1. **Core fields live in typed columns** (enforced at the database level)
    45â†’2. **Vertical-specific fields live in JSONB** (enforced at the application level via `normalizeDetails`)
    46â†’3. **Relationships are expressed through point IDs and spatial proximity** (not foreign keys between entities)
    47â†’
    48â†’### 1.2 Core Entity Schema (All Verticals)
    49â†’
    50â†’This extends the existing `point_events` and `snapshots` tables. No migration needed for the event table itself -- the category CHECK constraint is the only change.
    51â†’
    52â†’```sql
    53â†’-- Migration: extend category constraint to include all 7 target verticals
    54â†’ALTER TABLE point_events DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS point_events_category_check;
    55â†’ALTER TABLE point_events ADD CONSTRAINT point_events_category_check
    56â†’  CHECK (category IN (
    57â†’    'pharmacy',
    58â†’    'fuel_station',
    59â†’    'mobile_money',
    60â†’    'alcohol_outlet',
    61â†’    'billboard',
    62â†’    'transport_road',
    63â†’    'census_proxy'
    64â†’  ));
    65â†’```
    66â†’
    67â†’The universal entity fields (stored as typed columns in `point_events`):
    68â†’
    69â†’```
    70â†’point_events
    71â†’â”œâ”€â”€ id              uuid        PRIMARY KEY     -- event UUID
    72â†’â”œâ”€â”€ point_id        text        NOT NULL        -- entity identifier (see Section 2)
    73â†’â”œâ”€â”€ event_type      text        NOT NULL        -- 'CREATE_EVENT' | 'ENRICH_EVENT'
    74â†’â”œâ”€â”€ user_id         text        NOT NULL        -- contributor ID
    75â†’â”œâ”€â”€ category        text        NOT NULL        -- vertical ID
    76â†’â”œâ”€â”€ latitude        float8      NOT NULL        -- WGS84
    77â†’â”œâ”€â”€ longitude       float8      NOT NULL        -- WGS84
    78â†’â”œâ”€â”€ details         jsonb       NOT NULL        -- vertical-specific payload
    79â†’â”œâ”€â”€ photo_url       text                        -- primary evidence photo
    80â†’â”œâ”€â”€ created_at      timestamptz NOT NULL        -- event timestamp
    81â†’â”œâ”€â”€ source          text                        -- 'field_agent' | 'osm_overpass' | 'seed_import' | 'csv_import'
    82â†’â””â”€â”€ external_id     text                        -- dedup key for imports (e.g., 'osm_overpass:4382447689')
    83â†’```
    84â†’
    85â†’The projected entity (computed from events, stored in `snapshots` during weekly runs):
    86â†’
    87â†’```
    88â†’ProjectedPoint (TypeScript interface -- already exists)
    89â†’â”œâ”€â”€ id              string          -- same as pointId
    90â†’â”œâ”€â”€ pointId         string          -- stable entity ID
    91â†’â”œâ”€â”€ category        SubmissionCategory
    92â†’â”œâ”€â”€ location        { latitude, longitude }
    93â†’â”œâ”€â”€ details         SubmissionDetails   -- merged from all events
    94â†’â”œâ”€â”€ photoUrl        string | undefined
    95â†’â”œâ”€â”€ createdAt       string          -- first event timestamp
    96â†’â”œâ”€â”€ updatedAt       string          -- most recent event timestamp
    97â†’â”œâ”€â”€ source          string | undefined
    98â†’â”œâ”€â”€ externalId      string | undefined
    99â†’â”œâ”€â”€ gaps            string[]        -- missing enrichable fields
   100â†’â”œâ”€â”€ eventsCount     number          -- total events contributing
   101â†’â””â”€â”€ eventIds        string[]        -- all event UUIDs
   102â†’```

341â†’## 6. 80/20 Focus
   342â†’
   343â†’### 6.1 Which Verticals to Prioritize
   344â†’
   345â†’The top 20% of collection effort that produces 80% of commercial value:
   346â†’
   347â†’| Rank | Vertical | % of Collection Effort | % of Estimated Revenue | Revenue/Effort Ratio | Action |
   348â†’|---|---|---|---|---|---|
   349â†’| **1** | **Transport Roads** | 10% | 25% | **2.5x** | Allocate resources first; baseline before rainy season |
   350â†’| **2** | **Mobile Money** | 15% | 30% | **2.0x** | Highest absolute revenue; collect simultaneously with Roads |
   351â†’| **3** | **Fuel** | 5% | 10% | **2.0x** | Quick wins (only 5-10 stations); natural cross-sell with Roads |
   352â†’| 4 | Alcohol | 25% | 20% | 0.8x | Strong buyer interest but high collection difficulty |
   353â†’| 5 | Billboards | 10% | 8% | 0.8x | Fixed, visible, daytime -- easy but smaller market |
   354â†’| 6 | Pharmacy | 15% | 5% | 0.3x | High project-based value from WHO/USAID, slower sales cycle |
   355â†’| 7 | Census | 20% | 2% | 0.1x | Strategic long-term asset; collect passively during other surveys |
   356â†’
   357â†’**Conclusion:** Focus 30% of field agent time on Roads + Mobile Money + Fuel to capture 65% of estimated revenue. These are the 80/20 sweet spot.

   405â†’## 7. Validation SOP
   406â†’
   407â†’### 7.1 End-to-End Data Flow
   408â†’
   409â†’```
   410â†’CAPTURE --> VERIFY --> SCORE CONFIDENCE --> PUBLISH DELTA
   411â†’
   412â†’Step 1: CAPTURE (Field Agent, via PWA)
   413â†’  |
   414â†’  |  Agent selects vertical, takes photo, GPS auto-captured,
   415â†’  |  fills required fields (progressive disclosure)
   416â†’  |  GPS integrity report collected (mock location, sensors, accuracy)
   417â†’  |  Submission queued in IndexedDB (offline-first)
   418â†’  |
   419â†’  v
   420â†’Step 2: SYNC (Client --> Server)
   421â†’  |
   422â†’  |  IndexedDB queue flushes with idempotency keys
   423â†’  |  Exponential backoff on failure
   424â†’  |  Photo uploaded to Vercel Blob
   425â†’  |  Event written to point_events (CREATE_EVENT or ENRICH_EVENT)
   426â†’  |
   427â†’  v
   428â†’Step 3: VERIFY (Server-Side, Automated)
   429â†’  |
   430â†’  |  3a. GPS Validation Pipeline
   431â†’  |  3b. Photo Validation Pipeline
   432â†’  |  3c. Entity Deduplication
   433â†’  |  3d. Fraud Risk Scoring
   434â†’  |
   435â†’  v
   436â†’Step 4: SCORE CONFIDENCE
   437â†’  |
   438â†’  |  Composite confidence score computed
   439â†’  |  Auto-routing: approve / soft review / hard review / reject
   440â†’  |
   441â†’  v
   442â†’Step 5: ADMIN REVIEW (for flagged submissions)
   443â†’  |
   444â†’  |  Admin reviews photo, GPS, metadata, fraud flags
   445â†’  |  Approve, reject, or request re-submission
   446â†’  |
   447â†’  v
   448â†’Step 6: PUBLISH DELTA (Weekly)
   449â†’  |
   450â†’  |  Weekly snapshot frozen (Vercel Cron)
   451â†’  |  Delta computed: S(n) - S(n-1)
   452â†’  |  Z-score anomaly detection on delta stats
   453â†’  |  Published to API / dashboard / reports
   454â†’```
   455â†’
   456â†’### 7.2 Confidence Scoring Formula
   457â†’
   458â†’ADL uses two complementary scores:
   459â†’
   460â†’#### A. Data Quality Confidence Score (0-100)
   461â†’
   462â†’Measures how reliable a data point is. Computed per POI and updated with each event.
   463â†’
   464â†’```
   465â†’DATA QUALITY CONFIDENCE SCORE
   466â†’==============================
   467â†’
   468â†’Factor                Weight    Scoring
   469â†’--------------------------------------------------
   470â†’Recency               25%      100 if <7 days old, decays linearly to 0 at 90 days
   471â†’Source Count           20%      min(100, sourceCount * 25) -- more sources = higher confidence
   472â†’Photo Evidence         20%      100 if photo with EXIF GPS; 60 if photo without EXIF; 0 if no photo
   473â†’GPS Accuracy           15%      100 if <10m; 80 if <30m; 50 if <50m; 20 if <100m; 0 if >100m
   474â†’Reviewer Approval      10%      100 if admin approved; 50 if pending; 0 if rejected
   475â†’Field Completeness     10%      (filled fields / total fields) * 100
   476â†’
   477â†’FINAL SCORE = weighted sum of all factors, clamped to 0-100
   478â†’
   479â†’Publication threshold: >= 40 (below 40, data is withheld from client-facing products)
   480â†’High-confidence threshold: >= 70 (used for premium data products)
   481â†’```
   482â†’
   483â†’#### B. Fraud Risk Score (0-100)
   484â†’
   485â†’Measures how likely a submission is fraudulent. Computed at submission time.
   486â†’
   487â†’```
   488â†’FRAUD RISK SCORE
   489â†’=================
   490â†’
   491â†’Category              Weight    Signals
   492â†’--------------------------------------------------
   493â†’GPS Integrity          25%      Inverted GPS confidence (100 - GPS_CONFIDENCE_SCORE)
   494â†’                                Mock location detection, EXIF mismatch, IP mismatch,
   495â†’                                velocity analysis, sensor data
   496â†’
   497â†’Photo Integrity        25%      SHA-256 exact duplicate check (reject if different POI)
   498â†’                                Perceptual hash (aHash, Hamming distance <=5 = near-identical)
   499â†’                                EXIF fingerprint match (same device+time+GPS)
   500â†’                                Photo freshness (100 if <30 min, decays; <10 if >7 days)
   501â†’                                Screenshot detection, metadata editing detection
   502â†’
   503â†’Temporal Consistency   15%      Submission velocity (>200 km/h = block, >80 km/h = flag)
   504â†’                                Time-of-day plausibility
   505â†’                                Offline batch anomaly analysis
   506â†’
   507â†’Agent History          20%      Account age, prior fraud flags, prior rejections,
   508â†’                                trust tier (new/established/veteran),
   509â†’                                consecutive clean submissions
   510â†’
   511â†’Behavioral Patterns    15%      Self-enrichment patterns, device sharing,
   512â†’                                category distribution anomalies, IP reputation (VPN/datacenter)
   513â†’
   514â†’COMPOSITE FRAUD RISK = weighted sum, clamped to 0-100
   515â†’```
   516â†’
   517â†’#### C. Auto-Routing Rules
   518â†’
   519â†’| Fraud Risk Score | Data Quality Score | Action | XP |
   520â†’|---|---|---|---|
   521â†’| 0-20 | >= 70 | **Auto-approve** | Award immediately |
   522â†’| 0-20 | 40-69 | **Soft review** (admin reviews within 48h) | Escrow until review |
   523â†’| 21-50 | Any | **Soft review** | Escrow until review |
   524â†’| 51-70 | Any | **Hard review** (admin must investigate) | Deny until cleared |
   525â†’| 71-100 | Any | **Auto-reject** | Deny |
   526â†’
   527â†’### 7.3 Fraud Detection Checkpoints
   528â†’
   529â†’Each submission passes through these checkpoints in order:
   530â†’
   531â†’```
   532â†’CHECKPOINT 1: CLIENT-SIDE (before submission)
   533â†’  [ ] Mock location API check (Android isMocked flag)
   534â†’  [ ] GPS accuracy sanity (reject if accuracy = 0 or <1m on consumer device)
   535â†’  [ ] Sensor data collection (2 sec accelerometer sample)
   536â†’  [ ] Network type recording
   537â†’  [ ] GPS timestamp vs device clock check (flag if >30s drift)
   538â†’
   539â†’CHECKPOINT 2: SERVER-SIDE (at submission time)
   540â†’  [ ] EXIF GPS vs submission GPS (threshold: 1km standard, 2km low-end device)
   541â†’  [ ] IP geolocation vs submission GPS (threshold: 50km)
   542â†’  [ ] Velocity check (time + distance since last submission by same user)
   543â†’      - >200 km/h or >5km in <2 min = BLOCK
   544â†’      - >80 km/h = FLAG
   545â†’  [ ] Photo SHA-256 exact hash check against all prior submissions
   546â†’  [ ] Perceptual hash check (Hamming distance <=5 on aHash = flag)
   547â†’  [ ] EXIF fingerprint dedup (same device + time + GPS = flag)
   548â†’  [ ] Photo freshness check (>7 days old = reject; >24h = flag)
   549â†’  [ ] Screenshot detection (no EXIF + specific resolution patterns)
   550â†’  [ ] Entity proximity dedup (per-vertical thresholds, e.g., 10m for mobile money)
   551â†’  [ ] Geofence check (within Bonamoussadi: 4.0755-4.0999 N, 9.7185-9.7602 E)
   552â†’
   553â†’CHECKPOINT 3: BATCH ANALYSIS (daily cron)
   554â†’  [ ] Agent movement pattern analysis (cluster count, radius, precision distribution)
   555â†’  [ ] Cross-agent duplicate detection (different agents submitting same entity)
   556â†’  [ ] Collusion pattern detection (circular enrichment graphs)
   557â†’  [ ] Coordinate precision anomaly (>80% of submissions with <=4 decimal places = flag)
   558â†’  [ ] Alt account detection (same deviceId or IP across multiple userIds)
   559â†’```
   560â†’
   561â†’### 7.4 Review Workflow
   562â†’
   563â†’```
   564â†’ADMIN REVIEW QUEUE WORKFLOW
   565â†’=============================
   566â†’
   567â†’1. TRIAGE (daily, 9:00 AM WAT)
   568â†’   - Sort queue by fraud risk score (highest first)
   569â†’   - Auto-rejected items: log and archive (no action needed)
   570â†’   - Auto-approved items: log and publish (no action needed)
   571â†’   - Hard review items: investigate immediately
   572â†’   - Soft review items: batch review after hard reviews
   573â†’
   574â†’2. INVESTIGATION (per flagged submission)
   575â†’   - View side-by-side: photo | map (GPS pin) | submission data | fraud flags
   576â†’   - Check EXIF metadata panel (capture time, device, GPS)
   577â†’   - Compare with nearby existing POIs (dedup check)
   578â†’   - Review agent history (recent submissions, trust score, prior flags)
   579â†’   - If GPS flag: verify photo content matches the neighborhood
   580â†’   - If photo flag: compare flagged photo with matched photo(s)
   581â†’
   582â†’3. DECISION
   583â†’   - APPROVE: submission enters published dataset; XP released to agent
   584â†’   - REJECT: submission archived; XP denied; agent notified with reason
   585â†’   - REQUEST RE-SUBMISSION: agent asked to revisit and re-capture
   586â†’   - MERGE: duplicate submission merged with existing POI (enrichment)
   587â†’
   588â†’4. AGENT FEEDBACK
   589â†’   - Rejection reason communicated to agent via app notification
   590â†’   - Repeated rejections trigger trust score reduction
   591â†’   - 3+ confirmed fraud incidents = agent suspension pending investigation
   592â†’```
   593â†’
   594â†’### 7.5 Spot-Check Audit Protocol
   595â†’
   596â†’Physical field verification of data accuracy:
   597â†’
   598â†’```
   599â†’SPOT-CHECK AUDIT SOP
   600â†’=====================
   601â†’
   602â†’Frequency: Weekly (W3-W6), expanding scope each week
   603â†’  - Week 3: 10 random POIs
   604â†’  - Week 4: 10 different random POIs
   605â†’  - Week 5: 15 random POIs
   606â†’  - Week 6: 50 POIs (comprehensive, all verticals)
   607â†’
   608â†’Executor: Field Team Lead (independent of agents who submitted)
   609â†’
   610â†’For each audited POI:
   611â†’  1. Navigate to the GPS coordinates recorded in the submission
   612â†’  2. Verify EXISTENCE: Does the entity physically exist at this location? (Y/N)
   613â†’  3. Verify NAME: Does the name match what was submitted? (Y/N/Close)
   614â†’  4. Verify CATEGORY: Is the vertical classification correct? (Y/N)
   615â†’  5. Verify STATUS: Is the operating status correct (open/closed)? (Y/N)
   616â†’  6. Verify PHOTO: Does the submission photo match what you see? (Y/N)
   617â†’  7. Take independent verification photo
   618â†’  8. Note any discrepancies
   619â†’
   620â†’Scoring:
   621â†’  - MATCH: All 5 criteria verified (existence, name, category, status, photo)
   622â†’  - PARTIAL: 3-4 criteria match (acceptable, may need enrichment)
   623â†’  - MISMATCH: <3 criteria match (data quality failure)
   624â†’
   625â†’Audit accuracy = (MATCH + PARTIAL) / Total Audited * 100
   626â†’
   627â†’Threshold: >= 80% (W3), >= 85% (W4-W5), >= 85% (W6 comprehensive)
   628â†’
   629â†’If audit accuracy drops below 70%: PAUSE collection; retrain agents; investigate cause
   630â†’```
   631â†’
   632â†’### 7.6 Publication Criteria
   633â†’
   634â†’A data point is published (made available to clients via API/dashboard) when:
   635â†’
   636â†’```
   637â†’PUBLICATION CRITERIA
   638â†’=====================
   639â†’
   640â†’ALL of the following must be true:
   641â†’
   642â†’  [x] Data Quality Confidence Score >= 40
   643â†’  [x] Fraud Risk Score < 71 (not auto-rejected)
   644â†’  [x] Admin review status is "approved" or "auto-approved"
   645â†’  [x] Photo evidence is present

   669â†’## Summary
   670â†’
   671â†’This strategy is built on three pillars, as synthesized from all 9 teammate deliverables:
   672â†’
   673â†’**1. Ground truth that nobody else has.** No competitor maps the informal economy at street level with verified, GPS-located, photo-evidenced data points. In Cameroon, where 30-50% of GDP is informal and the last census is 21 years old, this gap is enormous. ADL fills it.
   674â†’
   675â†’**2. Consistent deltas that demonstrate change.** Weekly snapshots with computed deltas turn static maps into dynamic intelligence. The delta is the product -- not the map. Road blockages during rainy season, mobile money agent churn, new alcohol outlets opening -- these changes are what buyers pay for.
   676â†’
   677â†’**3. Immediately clear value for transactional buyers.** Every data product answers: "If I buy this, what money do I save or make this week?" For logistics companies: avoid a CFA 200K truck reroute. For telcos: identify 15 agent gaps before competitors fill them. For breweries: find 23 bars where they are not selling.
   678â†’
   679â†’**The 6-week pilot (April 14 - May 22, 2026) will answer one question:** Can ADL collect reliable, ground-truth data in Bonamoussadi at a cost (~$3,400 total) and quality level (>85% audit accuracy) that creates a product worth paying for?
   680â†’
   681â†’**Revenue trajectory if successful:**
   682â†’- Month 6: CFA 5-10M/month (2-3 paying customers at Professional tier)
   683â†’- Month 12: CFA 20-40M/month (1-2 enterprise + 5-8 professional tier)
   684â†’- Path to CFA 100M+/month: City-wide Douala coverage, enterprise telco and development org contracts

1660â†’## 6. Data Quality Scoring
  1661â†’
  1662â†’### 6.1 Confidence Score System
  1663â†’
  1664â†’The confidence score is a 0-100 integer that expresses how reliable a data point is. It is computed from multiple factors and stored in `details.confidenceScore`.
  1665â†’
  1666â†’### 6.2 Scoring Factors
  1667â†’
  1668â†’```
  1669â†’CONFIDENCE SCORE FACTORS
  1670â†’=========================
  1671â†’
  1672â†’Factor                    Weight    Range    Description
  1673â†’â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
  1674â†’recency                   25%       0-25     How recent is the last observation?
  1675â†’sourceCount               20%       0-20     How many independent events contribute?
  1676â†’photoEvidence             20%       0-20     Quality of photographic evidence
  1677â†’gpsAccuracy               15%       0-15     GPS precision and cross-validation
  1678â†’reviewerApproval          10%       0-10     Has an admin approved this data?
  1679â†’fieldCompleteness         10%       0-10     What % of enrichable fields are filled?
  1680â†’â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
  1681â†’TOTAL                    100%       0-100
  1682â†’```
  1683â†’
  1684â†’### 6.3 Score Computation Formula
  1685â†’
  1686â†’```typescript
  1687â†’interface ConfidenceFactors {
  1688â†’  recency: number;           // 0-25
  1689â†’  sourceCount: number;       // 0-20
  1690â†’  photoEvidence: number;     // 0-20
  1691â†’  gpsAccuracy: number;       // 0-15
  1692â†’  reviewerApproval: number;  // 0-10
  1693â†’  fieldCompleteness: number; // 0-10
  1694â†’}
  1695â†’
  1696â†’function computeConfidenceScore(point: ProjectedPoint, referenceDate: Date): number {
  1697â†’
  1698â†’  // â”€â”€ RECENCY (0-25) â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
  1699â†’  const daysSinceUpdate = (referenceDate.getTime() - new Date(point.updatedAt).getTime())
  1700â†’    / (24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
  1701â†’
  1702â†’  let recency: number;
  1703â†’  if (daysSinceUpdate <= 7)       recency = 25;  // This week
  1704â†’  else if (daysSinceUpdate <= 14) recency = 22;  // Last week
  1705â†’  else if (daysSinceUpdate <= 30) recency = 18;  // This month
  1706â†’  else if (daysSinceUpdate <= 60) recency = 12;  // Last month
  1707â†’  else if (daysSinceUpdate <= 90) recency = 6;   // 2-3 months ago
  1708â†’  else                            recency = 0;   // Stale
  1709â†’
  1710â†’  // â”€â”€ SOURCE COUNT (0-20) â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
  1711â†’  // Multiple independent observations increase confidence
  1712â†’  const events = point.eventsCount;
  1713â†’  let sourceCount: number;
  1714â†’  if (events >= 5)       sourceCount = 20;
  1715â†’  else if (events >= 3)  sourceCount = 15;
  1716â†’  else if (events >= 2)  sourceCount = 10;
  1717â†’  else                   sourceCount = 5;   // Single observation
  1718â†’
  1719â†’  // Bonus: if events come from different users (multi-source)
  1720â†’  // This requires checking event user_ids -- simplified here
  1721â†’  // as a bonus applied during projection
  1722â†’
  1723â†’  // â”€â”€ PHOTO EVIDENCE (0-20) â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
  1724â†’  let photoEvidence = 0;
  1725â†’  if (point.photoUrl) photoEvidence += 10;           // Has primary photo
  1726â†’  if (point.details.hasSecondaryPhoto) photoEvidence += 5;  // Has secondary photo
  1727â†’  if (point.details.fraudCheck) {
  1728â†’    const fc = point.details.fraudCheck as SubmissionFraudCheck;
  1729â†’    if (fc.primaryPhoto?.exifStatus === "ok") photoEvidence += 5; // EXIF validated
  1730â†’  }
  1731â†’

# 80/20 Analysis: High-Value Data Points

**Document:** workflow/06-eighty-twenty-focus.md
**Date:** March 2026
**Purpose:** Operational guide for field agents and team leads to maximize revenue impact per hour of collection effort

---

> **EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**
>
> Focus **30% of agent time** on **transport_road + mobile_money + fuel_station** to capture **65% of estimated revenue**.
>
> These three verticals have the highest revenue-to-effort ratios (2.0x--2.5x). Everything else is important but secondary. When time is short, collect Roads, MoMo, and Fuel first.

---

## 1. Revenue / Effort Ratio by Vertical

| Priority | Vertical | Collection Effort | Est. Revenue Share | Revenue/Effort Ratio | Action |
|:---:|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| **P0** | `transport_road` | 10% | 25% | **2.5x** | Allocate first; baseline before rainy season (June) |
| **P0** | `mobile_money` | 15% | 30% | **2.0x** | Highest absolute revenue; collect alongside roads |
| **P0** | `fuel_station` | 5% | 10% | **2.0x** | Quick wins (5--10 stations in Bonamoussadi); cross-sell with roads |
| P1 | `alcohol_outlet` | 25% | 20% | 0.8x | Strong buyer interest (SABC, breweries) but high collection difficulty |
| P1 | `billboard` | 10% | 8% | 0.8x | Fixed, visible, daytime collection -- easy but smaller market |
| P2 | `pharmacy` | 15% | 5% | 0.3x | High project-based value (WHO/USAID); slower sales cycle |
| P2 | `census_proxy` | 20% | 2% | 0.1x | Strategic long-term asset; collect passively during other surveys |

**Key takeaway:** P0 verticals consume 30% of effort and return 65% of revenue. P1 adds 35% effort for 28% revenue. P2 is strategic but not revenue-urgent.

---

## 2. Must-Have vs Nice-to-Have Fields (Field Agent Cheat Sheet)

Use this table when filling the capture form. **Must-Have fields are what buyers pay for.** If you only have 2 minutes at a location, capture the Must-Have fields and move on.

| Vertical | Must-Have Fields (buyers pay for these) | Nice-to-Have (add on revisit) |
|---|---|---|
| `transport_road` | Blockage status (`isBlocked`), vehicle passability (`passableBy`), condition, bypass route info | Surface type (`surfaceType`), traffic density (`trafficLevel`), street lights |
| `mobile_money` | Agent GPS location, operator (`providers`: MTN/Orange/both), activity status (`isActive`) | Float availability (`hasFloat`), services offered (`paymentModes`), agent type |
| `fuel_station` | Station GPS location, operator/brand, availability status (`hasFuelAvailable`) | Services offered, queue length (`queueLength`), informal vendor count, prices |
| `alcohol_outlet` | POS location, brands available (`brandsAvailable`), formal/informal (`isFormal`) | Operating hours, price range (`priceRange`), license status, outlet type |
| `billboard` | GPS location, advertiser/brand (`advertiserBrand`), occupancy status (`isOccupied`) | Format/size (`size`), condition, is lit (`isLit`), facing direction |
| `pharmacy` | GPS location, formal/informal (`isFormal`), operating status (`isOpenNow`) | On-duty rotation (`isOnDuty`), product categories, stock-out data, license |
| `census_proxy` | Building count, construction activity (`occupancyStatus`), building type | Storey count, occupancy, residential/commercial split, materials |

---

## 3. Highest-Value Delta Types

Deltas (changes between weekly snapshots) are the core product -- not static maps. Rank your re-visits to detect these changes first.

| Rank | Delta Type | Verticals Affected | Why Highest Value | Primary Buyer |
|:---:|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Blockage / disruption events** | `transport_road`, `fuel_station` (stock-outs) | Immediate operational impact; time-sensitive; saves CFA 50--200K per truck reroute avoided | Logistics companies, ride-hailing apps |
| **2** | **New entity openings** | `mobile_money`, `alcohol_outlet`, `pharmacy` | Distribution expansion signal; competitive intelligence; first-mover advantage | Telcos (MTN, Orange), breweries (SABC), pharma distributors |
| **3** | **Entity closures** | `mobile_money`, `alcohol_outlet` | Network health signal; competitor opportunity; market contraction indicator | Telcos, breweries |
| **4** | **Brand / operator switches** | `mobile_money`, `billboard` | Competitive market share shift signal | Telcos, advertising agencies |
| **5** | **Formal / informal ratio shifts** | `alcohol_outlet`, `pharmacy` | Regulatory environment signal; market maturation | Regulators, public health orgs, development agencies |
| **6** | **Density changes by zone** | All verticals | Neighborhood economic development signal; investment indicator | Banks, developers, telcos, government |

---

## 4. Six-Month Resource Allocation Calendar

Pilot starts April 2026. Rainy season begins June.

| Month | Primary Focus (60% of time) | Secondary Focus (25% of time) | Background (15% of time) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Month 1 (Apr)** | `transport_road` baseline (all segments) | `mobile_money` agent mapping | -- | Roads baseline must complete before rains; MoMo collected in parallel on same routes |
| **Month 2 (May)** | `mobile_money` deep collection (activity, services, float) | `fuel_station` complete inventory | `billboard` opportunistic | Deepen MoMo data for telco pitch; fuel is fast (5--10 stations) |
| **Month 3 (Jun)** | `transport_road` ACTIVE MONITORING (rains begin) | `alcohol_outlet` POS mapping starts | `pharmacy` opportunistic | Rainy season = highest-value road delta data; start alcohol for SABC pitch |
| **Month 4 (Jul)** | `transport_road` + `alcohol_outlet` + `billboard` | `mobile_money` weekly updates | `pharmacy` mapping | Multi-vertical coverage strengthens platform pitch to enterprise buyers |
| **Month 5 (Aug)** | All verticals -- weekly update cycles | `pharmacy` mapping completes | `census_proxy` pilot zones | System mature enough for full coverage; pharmacy data for development org pitches |
| **Month 6 (Sep)** | All verticals -- weekly updates | `census_proxy` baseline collection | Annual report preparation | Census starts as longitudinal baseline builds; prepare first client-ready reports |

### Time Allocation Summary (6-Month Average)

| Vertical | % of Agent Time | Estimated Entities (Bonamoussadi) |
|---|:---:|:---:|
| `transport_road` | 25% | 50--80 road segments |
| `mobile_money` | 20% | 100--200 agents |
| `fuel_station` | 5% | 5--10 stations |
| `alcohol_outlet` | 20% | 80--150 outlets |
| `billboard` | 10% | 30--60 billboards |
| `pharmacy` | 10% | 30--55 pharmacies |
| `census_proxy` | 10% | 560--800 buildings |

---

## 5. Field Agent Daily Decision Guide

**When time is short, follow this priority order.** Stop when your shift ends; the highest-priority items are at the top.

### Priority 1: Time-Sensitive Deltas (do these FIRST)

- [ ] Report any **road blockages** you encounter during transit (`transport_road`, `isBlocked` = true, `blockageType`, `passableBy`)
- [ ] Report any **fuel stock-outs** you observe at stations (`fuel_station`, `hasFuelAvailable` = false)
- [ ] Report any **new MoMo agent** you see that is not in the app (`mobile_money`, CREATE_EVENT)
- [ ] Report any **closed MoMo agent** that shows as active in the app (`mobile_money`, ENRICH_EVENT with `isActive` = false)

### Priority 2: Baseline Collection (do these if you have 30+ min)

- [ ] Walk your assigned route and capture any **unrecorded road segments** (`transport_road`, CREATE_EVENT)
- [ ] Capture all **MoMo agents** along your route with operator and activity status
- [ ] Capture any **fuel stations** you pass (quick -- just name, brand, availability)

### Priority 3: Enrichment (do these if you have 60+ min)

- [ ] Revisit MoMo agents with incomplete data (check `gaps` list in app) -- add float, services, hours
- [ ] Map `alcohol_outlet` locations with brands and formal/informal classification
- [ ] Map `billboard` locations with advertiser and occupancy status

### Priority 4: Background Collection (do these when convenient)

- [ ] Note `pharmacy` locations with open/closed and formal/informal status
- [ ] Count buildings and note construction activity for `census_proxy` (can be done from the street)

### Quick Rules of Thumb

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| You see a road blocked by flooding | **Stop everything.** Report immediately -- this is the highest-value data point. |
| You pass a fuel station | Takes 2 min. Capture name, brand, fuel availability. Always worth it. |
| You see a new MoMo kiosk | Takes 3 min. Capture location, operator, active status. High value. |
| You have 15 minutes left in shift | Do NOT start a new area. Instead, enrich existing points near you (fill gaps). |
| It is raining heavily | Focus on `transport_road` -- this is when blockage data is most valuable. |
| You are in a commercial zone | Prioritize `alcohol_outlet` and `mobile_money` -- high density, efficient collection. |
| You are on a main road | Prioritize `billboard` and `fuel_station` -- visible from the road, fast capture. |

---

## Sources

This document synthesizes and operationalizes content from the following research deliverables:

- `research/10-FINAL-STRATEGY-synthesis.md` -- Section 6 (80/20 Focus): vertical prioritization, revenue/effort ratios, resource allocation calendar, must-have vs nice-to-have fields, highest-value delta types
- `research/02-system-design-collection-workflow.md` -- Vertical-specific field schemas (Section 1.4), collection targets and entity estimates (Appendix), enrichable field definitions per vertical config
- `research/06-fraud-specialist-anti-fraud.md` -- Fraud threat landscape informing which data points require the most careful collection (GPS integrity, photo evidence requirements)

# Capture --> Verify --> Score --> Publish SOP

**Document:** workflow/07-validation-sop.md
**Date:** March 2026
**Purpose:** Standard Operating Procedure for the end-to-end data validation pipeline, from field capture to client-facing publication

---

## Quick Reference Card

Keep these five critical thresholds memorized:

| # | Threshold | Value | Meaning |
|:---:|---|:---:|---|
| 1 | **Publication minimum** | Quality Score **>= 40** | Below 40, data is withheld from all client-facing products |
| 2 | **High-confidence** | Quality Score **>= 70** | Qualifies for premium data products and auto-publish |
| 3 | **Auto-reject fraud** | Fraud Score **>= 71** | Submission automatically rejected; XP denied |
| 4 | **GPS reject** | Speed **> 200 km/h** | Impossible travel between consecutive submissions; auto-block |
| 5 | **Photo reject** | Photo age **> 7 days** | Photo older than 7 days is hard-rejected; >24h is flagged |

---

## Pipeline Flowchart

```
  FIELD AGENT (PWA)                    SERVER                           ADMIN
  ==================                   ======                           =====

  +------------------+
  | 1. CAPTURE       |
  | - Select vertical|
  | - Take photo     |
  | - GPS auto-lock  |
  | - Fill fields    |

           |
           v
  +------------------+
  | 2. CLIENT CHECKS |
  | - Mock GPS?      |
  | - GPS accuracy   |
  | - Sensor sample  |
  | - Network type   |
  +--------+---------+
           |
           v
  +------------------+
  | 3. UPLOAD / SYNC |       +-------------------------+
  | - IndexedDB queue+------>| 4. SERVER VALIDATION    |
  | - Idempotency key|       | - GPS pipeline          |
  | - Photo to Blob  |       | - Photo pipeline        |
  | - Exp. backoff   |       | - Entity dedup          |
  +------------------+       | - Fraud risk scoring    |
                             +------------+------------+
                                          |
                                          v
                             +-------------------------+
                             | 5. COMPOSITE SCORING    |
                             | - Quality Score (0-100) |
                             | - Fraud Score (0-100)   |
                             +------------+------------+
                                          |
                          +---------------+---------------+
                          |               |               |
                          v               v               v
                   +-----------+   +------------+   +-----------+
                   | AUTO-     |   | REVIEW     |   | AUTO-     |
                   | APPROVE   |   | QUEUE      |   | REJECT    |
                   | Fraud <21 |   | Fraud      |   | Fraud >=71|
                   | Qual >=70 |   | 21-70      |   +-----------+
                   +-----+-----+   +------+-----+
                         |                |
                         |                v
                         |   +-------------------------+
                         |   | 6. ADMIN REVIEW         |------> APPROVE
                         |   | - Photo + map + flags   |------> REJECT
                         |   | - Agent history check   |------> REQUEST RESUBMIT
                         |   | - Dedup comparison      |------> MERGE (dedup)
                         |   +-------------------------+
                         |                |
                         v                v
                   +---------------------------+
                   | 7. PUBLISH DELTA (Weekly)  |
                   | - Snapshot frozen (Friday) |
                   | - Delta = S(n) - S(n-1)   |
                   | - Z-score anomaly check    |
                   | - API + dashboard + report |
                   +---------------------------+
```

---

## SOP Steps

### Step 1: Capture (Field Agent)

- [ ] 1.1 Open the PWA and select the correct vertical from the 7 categories: `billboard`, `alcohol_outlet`, `census_proxy`, `fuel_station`, `mobile_money`, `pharmacy`, `transport_road`
- [ ] 1.2 Take a photo of the entity (storefront, kiosk, road segment, billboard face, building)
    - [ ] Use the device camera directly (not gallery upload) -- this preserves EXIF data
    - [ ] Ensure the entity name/signage is visible in the photo when possible
    - [ ] For `transport_road`: photograph the road condition or blockage, not just the surroundings
- [ ] 1.3 Wait for GPS lock (the app auto-captures coordinates)
    - [ ] GPS accuracy indicator should show green (<30m) or yellow (<100m)
    - [ ] Do NOT submit if GPS shows red (>100m) -- wait or move to open sky
- [ ] 1.4 Fill required fields (the app shows them via progressive disclosure)
    - [ ] Must-Have fields are marked with an asterisk (*)
    - [ ] Fill Nice-to-Have fields if time permits (see `workflow/06-eighty-twenty-focus.md`)
- [ ] 1.5 Submit -- the event is queued in IndexedDB for offline-first reliability
    - [ ] A GPS integrity report is silently collected (mock location check, sensor sample, network type)
    - [ ] The submission gets an idempotency key to prevent duplicates on retry

### Step 2: Client-Side Checks (Automatic, in PWA)

These run transparently before the submission is queued:

- [ ] 2.1 Mock location API check (Android `isMocked` flag -- Chrome 90+)
- [ ] 2.2 GPS accuracy sanity: flag if accuracy = 0 or < 1m on consumer device
- [ ] 2.3 Sensor data collection: 2-second accelerometer sample to verify physical movement
- [ ] 2.4 Network type recording (`wifi`, `4g`, `3g`, `2g`)
- [ ] 2.5 GPS timestamp vs device clock: flag if drift > 30 seconds

**Note:** Client-side checks are bypassable by a determined attacker. They raise the bar and collect signals for server-side analysis.

### Step 3: Upload / Sync (Automatic)

- [ ] 3.1 IndexedDB queue flushes when connectivity is available
- [ ] 3.2 Exponential backoff on failure (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s)
- [ ] 3.3 Photo uploaded to Vercel Blob storage
- [ ] 3.4 Event written to `point_events` table (`CREATE_EVENT` or `ENRICH_EVENT`)
- [ ] 3.5 Idempotency key prevents duplicate writes on retry

### Step 4: Server-Side Validation (Automatic)

All checks run at submission time. Each check produces flags with severity levels (`info`, `warning`, `critical`).

#### 4a. GPS Validation Pipeline

- [ ] 4a.1 EXIF GPS vs submission GPS (threshold: 1km standard, 2km for low-end devices)
- [ ] 4a.2 IP geolocation vs submission GPS (threshold: 50km)
- [ ] 4a.3 Velocity check (time + distance since last submission by same agent):
    - `> 200 km/h` or `> 5km in < 2 min` --> **BLOCK** (auto-reject)
    - `> 80 km/h` --> **FLAG** (queue for review)
    - `<= 80 km/h` --> OK
- [ ] 4a.4 Geofence check: submission must fall within collection zone (Bonamoussadi: 4.0755--4.0999 N, 9.7185--9.7602 E)

#### 4b. Photo Validation Pipeline

- [ ] 4b.1 SHA-256 exact hash check: reject if same photo used for a DIFFERENT entity
- [ ] 4b.2 Perceptual hash (aHash): Hamming distance <= 5 on 64-bit hash = near-duplicate, flag for review
- [ ] 4b.3 EXIF fingerprint dedup: same device + capture time + GPS = flag
- [ ] 4b.4 Photo freshness: **> 7 days old = reject**; > 24 hours old = flag
- [ ] 4b.5 Screenshot detection: no EXIF + specific resolution patterns = flag

#### 4c. Entity Deduplication

- [ ] 4c.1 Proximity check: same vertical within dedup radius (default 25m; roads 50m)
- [ ] 4c.2 Name similarity check for same-vertical entities nearby
- [ ] 4c.3 If duplicate detected: route to merge workflow (not rejection)

#### 4d. Fraud Risk Scoring

Composite fraud risk score computed from 5 categories (see Section "Fraud Risk Score" below).

### Step 5: Composite Scoring

Two independent scores are computed for every submission:

#### A. Data Quality Confidence Score (0--100)

Measures how reliable a data point is. Updated with each event.

```
QUALITY SCORE FORMULA
======================

Factor               Weight    Scoring Rule
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Recency               25%      25 pts if <7d; 22 if <14d; 18 if <30d;
                               12 if <60d; 6 if <90d; 0 if older
Source Count           20%      20 pts if >=5 events; 15 if >=3;
                               10 if >=2; 5 if single observation
Photo Evidence         20%      10 pts primary photo + 5 pts secondary
                               photo + 5 pts if EXIF GPS validated
GPS Accuracy           15%      5 pts base (GPS present) + 5 pts if
                               photo GPS matches + 5 pts if IP matches
Reviewer Approval      10%      10 pts if admin approved; 3 pts if
                               >=2 events but unreviewed; 0 otherwise
Field Completeness     10%      (filled enrichable fields / total
                               enrichable fields) * 10

TOTAL = sum of all factors, clamped to 0-100
```

**Score thresholds:**

| Score | Label | Action |
|:---:|---|---|
| 80--100 | HIGH CONFIDENCE | Auto-publish, no review needed |
| 60--79 | GOOD | Publish, review within 72h |
| 40--59 | MODERATE | Publish with caveat, review within 48h |
| 20--39 | LOW | Do NOT publish; queue for review |
| 0--19 | VERY LOW | Flag for investigation; possible fraud |

#### B. Fraud Risk Score (0--100)

Measures how likely a submission is fraudulent. Computed at submission time.

```
FRAUD RISK SCORE FORMULA
==========================

Category              Weight    Signals
-----------------------------------------------------------------
GPS Integrity          25%      Inverted GPS confidence score
                               (100 - GPS_CONFIDENCE). Factors:
                               mock location, EXIF mismatch,
                               IP mismatch, velocity, sensors
Photo Integrity        25%      SHA-256 exact duplicate check,
                               perceptual hash (aHash, dist <=5),
                               EXIF fingerprint match, photo
                               freshness (>7d = max risk),
                               screenshot detection
Temporal Consistency   15%      Velocity (>200 km/h = block),
                               time-of-day plausibility,
                               offline batch anomaly analysis
Agent History          20%      Account age, prior fraud flags,
                               prior rejections, trust tier
                               (new / established / veteran),
                               consecutive clean submissions
Behavioral Patterns    15%      Self-enrichment ratio, device
                               sharing across accounts,
                               category distribution anomalies,
                               IP reputation (VPN/datacenter)

TOTAL = weighted sum, clamped to 0-100
```

### Step 6: Auto-Routing Decision

The combination of Fraud Risk Score and Quality Score determines the routing action:

| Fraud Risk Score | Quality Score | Route | XP Action |
|:---:|:---:|---|---|
| 0--20 | >= 70 | **Auto-approve** | Award XP immediately |
| 0--20 | 40--69 | **Soft review** (admin reviews within 48h) | XP held in escrow |
| 21--50 | Any | **Soft review** (admin reviews within 48h) | XP held in escrow |
| 51--70 | Any | **Hard review** (admin must investigate) | XP denied until cleared |
| 71--100 | Any | **Auto-reject** | XP denied |

### Step 7: Admin Review Queue Workflow

#### 7.1 Daily Triage (9:00 AM WAT)

- [ ] Sort the review queue by fraud risk score, highest first
- [ ] Auto-rejected items: verify the log entry, archive (no further action)
- [ ] Auto-approved items: verify the log entry, publish (no further action)
- [ ] **Hard review** items: investigate immediately (see 7.2)
- [ ] **Soft review** items: batch-review after all hard reviews are complete

#### 7.2 Investigation (Per Flagged Submission)

- [ ] View side-by-side: submitted photo | map with GPS pin | submission data | fraud flags
- [ ] Check EXIF metadata panel: capture timestamp, device make/model, GPS coordinates
- [ ] Compare with nearby existing POIs (dedup check -- same name? same photo?)
- [ ] Review agent history: recent submissions, trust score, prior flags, rejection count
- [ ] If GPS flag: does the photo content match the Bonamoussadi neighborhood?
- [ ] If photo flag: compare the flagged photo with the matched photo(s)

#### 7.3 Decision

| Decision | Effect | Agent Impact |
|---|---|---|
| **APPROVE** | Submission enters published dataset | XP released; trust score maintained |
| **REJECT** | Submission archived permanently | XP denied; agent notified with reason; trust score reduced |
| **REQUEST RE-SUBMISSION** | Agent asked to revisit and re-capture | XP held; agent sees specific re-capture instructions |
| **MERGE** | Duplicate submission merged with existing POI as enrichment | Partial XP awarded for enrichment contribution |

#### 7.4 Agent Feedback Loop

- [ ] Rejection reason communicated to the agent via in-app notification
- [ ] Repeated rejections trigger automatic trust score reduction
- [ ] **3 or more confirmed fraud incidents** = agent suspension pending investigation
- [ ] Agents can appeal rejections through the team lead

---

## Spot-Check Audit Protocol

Physical field verification of published data accuracy, conducted independently of the agents who submitted the data.

### Schedule

| Week | Sample Size | Scope |
|:---:|:---:|---|
| Week 3 | 10 random POIs | Initial calibration -- check basic accuracy |
| Week 4 | 10 different random POIs | Expand sample; different zones |
| Week 5 | 15 random POIs | Broader coverage; all active verticals |
| Week 6 | 50 POIs | Comprehensive audit across all verticals and zones |

**Executor:** Field Team Lead (must be independent of the agents who submitted the data being audited)

### Per-POI Audit Checklist

For each audited POI:

- [ ] 1. Navigate to the GPS coordinates recorded in the submission
- [ ] 2. **EXISTENCE:** Does the entity physically exist at this location? (Y / N)
- [ ] 3. **NAME:** Does the name match what was submitted? (Y / N / Close)
- [ ] 4. **CATEGORY:** Is the vertical classification correct? (Y / N)
- [ ] 5. **STATUS:** Is the operating status correct (open/closed/blocked)? (Y / N)
- [ ] 6. **PHOTO:** Does the submission photo match what you see? (Y / N)
- [ ] 7. Take an independent verification photo
- [ ] 8. Note any discrepancies in the audit log

### Scoring

| Result | Criteria | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| **MATCH** | All 5 criteria verified (existence, name, category, status, photo) | Data point is reliable |
| **PARTIAL** | 3--4 criteria match | Acceptable; may need enrichment event |
| **MISMATCH** | Fewer than 3 criteria match | Data quality failure; investigate root cause |

### Accuracy Thresholds

```
Audit Accuracy = (MATCH + PARTIAL) / Total Audited * 100

Week 3:          >= 80% required
Week 4-5:        >= 85% required
Week 6:          >= 85% required (comprehensive)

CIRCUIT BREAKER: If accuracy drops below 70%:
  --> PAUSE all collection
  --> Retrain agents
  --> Investigate root cause (fraud? training gap? UX confusion?)
  --> Resume only after remediation
```

---

## Publication Criteria

A data point is published (made available to clients via API/dashboard) when ALL of the following are true:

### Standard Publication

- [ ] Data Quality Confidence Score >= 40
- [ ] Fraud Risk Score < 71 (not auto-rejected)
- [ ] Admin review status is `approved` or `auto-approved`
- [ ] Photo evidence is present
- [ ] GPS coordinates are within the designated collection zone
- [ ] At least one required field per vertical is populated

### Premium / High-Confidence Publication

All standard criteria, PLUS:

- [ ] Data Quality Confidence Score >= 70
- [ ] Fraud Risk Score < 21
- [ ] Photo has EXIF GPS matching submission GPS (within 1km)
- [ ] At least one spot-check audit has verified this POI (or same-vertical POIs in same zone)
- [ ] At least one enrichment event exists (data confirmed by multiple sources)

### Delta Publication Cadence

- Weekly snapshot frozen every **Friday at 23:59 WAT** (Vercel Cron)
- Delta computed: new POIs, changed fields, removed/closed entities
- Z-score anomaly detection flags statistical outliers for manual review before publication
- Delta published to API endpoint: `/api/deltas?snapshot=S{n}`
- Client notifications sent for subscribed verticals/zones
- During rainy season (June--October): `transport_road` deltas computed **daily**
